metadata.summary: Pin pnpm via packageManager + corepack, commit pnpm-lock.yaml, avoid shamefully-hoist=true. Fix phantom deps via .pnpmfile.cjs, use workspace:* protocol, and enforce --frozen-lockfile in CI.

content.title: pnpm Package Management
content.summary: Covers pnpm configuration, workspace management, CI/CD integration, and security practices for JavaScript/TypeScript projects. Key rule: pin the pnpm version via `packageManager` field + `corepack`, always commit `pnpm-lock.yaml`, and never use `shamefully-hoist=true` — fix phantom deps via `.pnpmfile.cjs` instead.

why.p: pnpm's content-addressable store and strict non-hoisted layout prevent phantom dependency bugs and supply-chain confusion attacks that npm/yarn permit. Without pinned toolchain (`corepack`), version drift causes lockfile churn and "works on my machine" failures in CI. `--frozen-lockfile` in CI catches accidental dependency mutations.

list.item: All new JavaScript/TypeScript projects (single-package or monorepo).
CI/CD pipeline setup needing fast, reproducible installs.
Migrating an npm/yarn repo to pnpm.
Repos that mandate npm or yarn (legacy CI, customer constraint, polyrepo with hoisting deps).
Electron or legacy native modules that assume hoisted layout.
Projects using Bun or Deno as primary runtime where pnpm adds debug surface.

rule.statement: Pin the pnpm version in the root package.json `packageManager` field and activate via `corepack enable`.
rule.rationale: Without pinning, each developer and CI runner uses a different pnpm version, producing lockfile churn and "works on my machine" failures.

example.title: Pinned pnpm via packageManager
example.note: `preinstall` script blocks npm/yarn from being used accidentally.

rule.statement: Keep `shamefully-hoist=false` (the default). Fix phantom dep issues via `.pnpmfile.cjs` `readPackage` hook, never by hoisting.
rule.rationale: `shamefully-hoist=true` defeats pnpm's core benefit of preventing phantom dependencies and makes the project behave like npm.

example.title: Core pnpm dependency commands
example.code: pnpm add express zod # Production dep pnpm add -D typescript vitest # Dev dep pnpm add --save-peer react # Peer dep pnpm remove express # Remove pnpm update --latest # Update to latest (ignore semver) pnpm outdated # Show outdated pnpm audit --prod # Audit production deps only pnpm why lodash # Trace why transitive dep is installed

rule.statement: Reference internal packages with the `workspace:*` protocol; use Changesets to rewrite to real versions at publish time.
rule.rationale: `workspace:*` is resolved by pnpm locally but rewritten by Changesets on publish. Bypassing Changesets can leak unresolved `workspace:*` references to npm.

example.title: pnpm --filter for monorepos
example.code: pnpm --filter web build # Run in specific package pnpm --filter @scope/ui build # Run in scoped package pnpm --filter "./packages/*" build # Run in all packages under packages/ pnpm --filter web... build # Run in package and its deps pnpm -r build # Run in all packages (topological order) pnpm -r --parallel build # Run in all packages in parallel pnpm --filter web add react # Add dep to specific workspace pnpm --filter web add @scope/ui --workspace # Add internal workspace dep
example.note: Always use `--filter` for monorepo operations; bare `pnpm add` at a sub-package root without --filter installs to the wrong place.

section.name: GitHub Actions

rule.statement: Use `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in CI. Cache the pnpm store keyed by `pnpm-lock.yaml` hash.
rule.rationale: `--frozen-lockfile` fails if the lockfile is out of sync, catching accidental dependency mutations. Caching the store drastically reduces install time.

section.reference: GitHub Actions workflow with pnpm cache

example.title: Multi-stage Dockerfile with pnpm cache
example.code:
FROM node:20-slim AS base ENV PNPM_HOME="/pnpm" ENV PATH="$PNPM_HOME:$PATH" RUN corepack enable
FROM base AS deps WORKDIR /app COPY pnpm-lock.yaml ./ RUN --mount=type=cache,id=pnpm,target=/pnpm/store pnpm fetch
FROM deps AS build COPY . . RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --offline RUN pnpm build
FROM base AS runtime WORKDIR /app COPY --from=build /app/dist ./dist COPY --from=build /app/node_modules ./node_modules CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]
example.note: `pnpm fetch` pre-populates the store; `--offline` install uses it without network. Mount-cache persists across builds.

antipattern.description: Running `npm install` or `yarn add` in a pnpm repo, creating a `package-lock.json` or `yarn.lock` alongside pnpm-lock.yaml.
antipattern.reason: Mixed lockfiles cause non-deterministic installs and CI failures. Enforce with `preinstall: "npx only-allow pnpm"` and add `package-lock.json` / `yarn.lock` to `.gitignore`.
Without a committed lockfile, CI installs different versions from development, causing "works on my machine" bugs. Always commit the lockfile.

antipattern.description: Setting `shamefully-hoist=true` to fix a "module not found" error.
antipattern.reason: Hoisting hides phantom dependency bugs and makes the project behave like npm. Fix phantom deps by adding the missing dep explicitly with `pnpm add`.

antipattern.description: Editing package.json dependencies by hand instead of using `pnpm add` / `pnpm remove`.
antipattern.reason: Manual edits bypass peer-dependency resolution, producing an inconsistent lockfile. Always use pnpm CLI commands.

antipattern.description: Using `pnpm audit` in a retry loop when no fix exists for a transitive CVE.
antipattern.reason: `pnpm audit` exits non-zero even for CVEs with no fix. Use `pnpm audit --prod` to skip dev deps; check reachability with `pnpm why`; pin via `.pnpmfile.cjs` if truly reachable and unfixed.

antipattern.description: Using `pnpm add x` at workspace root without `--filter` intending to add to a sub-package.
antipattern.reason: Without --filter, the dep installs at the root, not the intended package. Always scope with `--filter <pkg-name>`.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Pin the toolchain first: agent reads/writes `packageManager` in root `package.json` and `engines`. Use `corepack` so the version is determined from the file, not globally installed. For installs, prefer `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in CI and `pnpm install` only in dev. For dependency changes the agent runs `pnpm add`/`remove` (never edits `package.json` by hand — easy to break peer-dep resolution). For monorepos, `pnpm --filter <pkg>` scopes the change. Always commit `pnpm-lock.yaml`. After updates, run `pnpm audit --prod` and `pnpm outdated` and surface results to a human reviewer.

section.name: Recommended Tools and Services
section.p: Tools: `pnpm` (the package manager itself), `corepack` (pin pnpm version per repo), `npx only-allow pnpm` (block non-pnpm installs in preinstall), `pnpm dlx` (like npx but uses pnpm store), `pnpm why` (trace why a transitive dep is installed), `pnpm licenses list` (inventory licences for compliance), `pnpm patch` (patch a dep), `pnpm dedupe` (reduce duplicates after upgrades), Changesets (versioning + publishing in workspaces), Renovate / Dependabot (automated dep upgrade PRs), npm-check-updates / ncu (bulk semver-major upgrades on demand), syncpack (enforce consistent versions across workspaces), nx / turbo (task graph aware of pnpm workspaces), verdaccio (local registry for testing publishes).

Services: GitHub Actions `pnpm/action-setup` (standard CI install, supports caching), Renovate (SaaS + OSS, best-in-class for pnpm monorepo upgrades), Dependabot (native to GitHub, solid pnpm support in 2026), Snyk (CVE scanning with reachability analysis), Socket.dev (supply-chain risk score on every PR), npm registry / GitHub Packages / Verdaccio (pin via `.npmrc`), Turborepo Remote Cache (speeds CI dramatically), Nx Cloud (distributed task execution), Changesets bot (enforces changeset on each PR).

section.name: Best Practices
section.p: Pin pnpm via `packageManager` field + `corepack` — prevents "works on my machine" version drift. Always commit `pnpm-lock.yaml`. CI uses `--frozen-lockfile`. PRs that change deps must include the lockfile diff. Keep `shamefully-hoist=false`. If a package is broken, fix it via `.pnpmfile.cjs` `readPackage` hook, not by hoisting the whole tree. In monorepos, prefer `workspace:*` over fixed versions for internal packages; Changesets handles the rewrite at publish. Use `pnpm dedupe` after major upgrades — pnpm doesn't auto-dedupe like npm. Cache the pnpm store in CI keyed by `pnpm-lock.yaml` hash. Run `pnpm audit --prod` (skip dev) — most CVEs in dev deps don't ship. Group dep updates: Renovate `groupName: 'eslint'` to land them in fewer PRs the agent can review at once.

section.name: AI-Agent Gotchas
section.p: Mixing managers: Agent runs `npm install` in a pnpm repo "to be safe", creates `package-lock.json`, breaks workflow. Add `preinstall: "npx only-allow pnpm"` and a `.gitignore` entry for `package-lock.json`/`yarn.lock`. Hand-editing package.json: Agent edits `dependencies` directly; peer-dep resolution lies. Force `pnpm add`/`remove` only. Phantom deps: Code uses a transitive dep that pnpm hides. Agent "fixes" by hoisting; correct fix is `pnpm add` the dep explicitly. Lockfile thrash: `corepack` version drift causes a re-keyed lockfile every PR. Pin pnpm exactly in `packageManager`. Workspace mis-targeting: `pnpm add x` at root vs in a sub-package installs in the wrong place. Always pass `--filter`. Workspace leaking: Manually publishing without Changesets can publish unresolved `workspace:*` to npm. Enforce Changesets-driven release. pnpm audit loop: No-fix CVE → agent retries forever. Cap retries; escalate to human. Permissions / symlinks: Agent sandbox without symlink perms (some Docker setups) silently degrades. Pre-flight with `pnpm doctor`. Human-in-loop checkpoint: Lockfile-changing PRs (especially major bumps) get a human review; agents have a poor track record on breaking-change reading.

template.purpose: Init script that pins toolchain and creates workspace scaffold
GitHub Actions workflow with pnpm cache
